# 2026-06-10 18:48:50Z

<!-- Cursor IDE Session 85b033fc-4eba-47c8-98e5-a3fc487eab10 (2026-06-10 18:48:50Z) -->

_**User**_

S006
Update the Rulesync command files so each command carries its own agent-like persona and operating style.

Goal:
Avoid adding more subagents. Instead, make the command files self-contained and explicit about how the AI should behave when running that command.

Please update only the relevant files under:

.rulesync/commands/

Do not change product docs.
Do not change implementation code.
Do not add new agents unless absolutely necessary.

For each command, add a short "Operating persona" section near the top.

Use these personas:

1. discovery.md
Persona: Product discovery analyst
Behaviour:
- extracts only what is supported by source documents
- separates facts, assumptions, open questions, and dropped scope
- does not invent product features
- keeps the requirement document as binding source of truth

2. prd.md
Persona: Lean product manager
Behaviour:
- turns discovery into a concise PRD
- focuses on customer outcomes and non-goals
- preserves requirement traceability
- avoids technical implementation details
- avoids scope creep

3. tech-spec.md
Persona: Pragmatic solution designer
Behaviour:
- translates approved PRD requirements into the smallest technical design that satisfies them
- prioritises traceability, simplicity, testability, and implementation sequencing
- avoids enterprise architecture, CQRS, event sourcing, microservices, unnecessary service layers, or speculative abstractions
- defines implementation slices mapped to PRD R* IDs

4. test-plan.md
Persona: Behaviour-focused test strategist
Behaviour:
- designs tests that protect real customer and regulatory behaviours
- prioritises edge cases, accessibility, data handling, and meaningful failures
- avoids happy-path-only tests and brittle implementation-detail tests
- maps tests back to PRD R* and tech-spec S* IDs

5. implement.md
Persona: Pragmatic TDD implementer
Behaviour:
- implements one approved tech-spec slice at a time
- reads PRD R*, tech-spec S*, and test-plan T* references first
- writes failing tests before implementation where practical
- implements only enough code to pass the tests
- refactors only after tests pass
- avoids expanding scope beyond the requested slice

6. review / critic command, if present
Persona: Critical reviewer
Behaviour:
- reviews read-only unless explicitly asked to edit
- identifies drift, missing requirements, over-engineering, and weak testability
- separates must-fix, should-fix, defer, and ignore
- does not rewrite documents by default

Also update any command wording that conflicts with these behaviours.

After editing:
1. Summarise each command changed.
2. Confirm no product or implementation files were changed.
3. Recommend the Rulesync generate command to run next.

---

| S004 | PRD generation | &#96;.specstory/history/2026-06-10_16-33-38Z-product-requirements-document-guidelines.md&#96; | &#96;docs/ai/sessions/S004-prd.md&#96; | &#96;0cca832&#96; on &#96;doc/prd&#96; | &#96;docs/PRD.md&#96; produced per &#96;docs/PRD_TEMPLATE.md&#96; structure, using only the brief PDF and &#96;docs/discovery/NOTES.md&#96; as authoritative sources (withdrawn &#96;docs/TASK_ANALYSIS.md&#96; not cited); lean MVP scope with **R1–R17** (Must: R1–R4 Core + R14–R17 Submission; Should: R5–R10 Vulnerability/Regulatory; Could: R11–R13 Stretch); persistence committed at PRD level only (R3) with technology choice deferred to tech-spec; **N1–N8** non-goals enforce the §7(b) drops in PRD itself (no arrangements endpoint, no agent UI, no collections workflow, no auto-vulnerability classification); **Q1↔A1, Q2↔A2, Q3↔A3** — every open question paired with a working-assumption default (band thresholds default to DI &gt; 0 / = 0 / &lt; 0; first-snapshot delta uses a friendly placeholder; irregular income accepted per-month as-entered); FCA / GDPR paraphrases inherited from &#96;NOTES.md&#96; with "_not independently verified_" labels intact (OQ-7); Discovery → PRD traceability table closes every &#96;NOTES.md&#96; insight to one or more &#96;R*&#96; IDs |
| S005 | PRD critic review + minimum append-only follow-ups | &#96;.specstory/history/2026-06-10_17-12-45Z-document-review-findings-and-recommendations.md&#96; | &#96;docs/ai/sessions/S005-prd-critic-and-followups.md&#96; | (in progress) on &#96;doc/prd&#96; | Critic role (&#96;.rulesync/subagents/critic.md&#96;) reviewed &#96;docs/PRD.md&#96; across 7 focus areas — Verdict: **Minor fixes**, 24 findings recorded; user accepted 4 for append-only resolution (**F3.1** accessibility gap, **F1.1** stretch-test discipline, **F4.1** retention assumption, **F7.2** snapshot correction story); PRD modified append-only: **R18** (Should / Vulnerability — screen-reader + motor accessibility; conformance level deferred to tech-spec to avoid fabricating a WCAG citation), **R19** (Should / Core — stretch tests if any of R11/R12/R13 are delivered; doesn't edit R4), **Q4↔A4** (retention defaults to lifetime-of-customer-record for take-home; production retention out-of-scope per N8), **Q5↔A5** (corrections = new snapshot; prior snapshots remain visible; UI "looks like a correction" hint deferred to tech-spec); §9 traceability extended for R18 / R19 only (Q/A pairs follow the established Q1–Q3 convention of no §9 row); R1–R17, Q1–Q3, A1–A3, §1–§5, §7 unchanged; PRD passes &#96;/tech-spec&#96; pre-checks; ~20 critic findings consciously deferred — categorised in S005 snapshot as (a) tech-spec / test-plan responsibility, (b) would edit existing rows (user constraint), (c) would introduce new product scope (user constraint), (d) cosmetic; deferred set is the inbox for &#96;/tech-spec&#96; and &#96;/test-plan&#96; |

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7)**_

All five command files now carry their own self-contained operating persona. Here's the rundown.

## Per-command summary of changes

### 1. `.rulesync/commands/discovery.md` — Product discovery analyst
Added an `## Operating persona` block stating the AI extracts only what the cited source documents support, keeps **facts / assumptions / open questions / dropped scope** as distinct sections, treats the brief as the binding upstream source and committed `docs/discovery/NOTES.md` as the authoritative discovery record, escalates rather than guesses, and refuses to draft PRD-style requirements in this command.

```9:19:.rulesync/commands/discovery.md
## Operating persona

**Product discovery analyst.** When running `/discovery`, the AI:

- extracts only what is supported by the cited source documents (the brief, prior committed notes, and links the user supplies in this session); does **not** invent product features, user research, or regulatory citations.
- keeps **facts**, **assumptions**, **open questions**, and **dropped scope** in distinct sections — never blurs them into one narrative.
- treats the brief as the binding upstream source of truth for scope at this phase, and treats `docs/discovery/NOTES.md` as the authoritative discovery record once committed.
- escalates rather than guesses: if a section cannot be filled from the sources, it goes under "Assumptions and open questions" as a `TBC` item with a one-line note on what is needed to resolve it.
- refuses to draft PRD-style requirements, acceptance criteria, or implementation choices in this command — those belong to later phases.
```

### 2. `.rulesync/commands/prd.md` — Lean product manager
Added an `## Operating persona` block focusing on customer outcomes and non-goals, banning implementation details (framework / library / schema / endpoint / storage / UI-component choices), preserving append-only `R*` IDs (with `R{n} — DROPPED (reason)` for removals), and codifying the existing convention of preferring an explicit `Q*` / `A*` pair over an invented requirement when discovery is silent.

### 3. `.rulesync/commands/tech-spec.md` — Pragmatic solution designer
Three changes:
- Added `## Operating persona`: smallest design that satisfies the PRD; **explicitly bans CQRS, event sourcing, microservices, speculative service layers, premature interfaces, and "future-proof" extension points**; each `S*` is sized to land in a single `/implement` session; deferred items go to **Out of scope** or **Trade-offs and alternatives considered**; true requirement gaps route back to `/prd`.
- §3 heading reworded **"Detailed sections" → "Implementation slices"**, with a "split if too large" clause.
- §6 rule extended so unjustified architectural layers (not just dependencies) must land in **Trade-offs and alternatives considered**, not silently disappear.

### 4. `.rulesync/commands/test-plan.md` — Behaviour-focused test strategist
Added an `## Operating persona` block: protects real customer and regulatory behaviours over code shape; prioritises edge cases, accessibility, data handling/privacy, and meaningful failure modes; explicitly avoids brittle implementation-detail tests (internal call counts, private helpers, DOM structure beyond what behaviour requires, framework internals); every `T*` maps back to both `R*` and `S*`; refuses to test behaviours the PRD does not require.

### 5. `.rulesync/commands/implement.md` — Pragmatic TDD implementer
Two changes:
- Added `## Operating persona`: one approved slice per invocation; reads `R*` / `S*` / `T*` before touching code; **failing tests first → minimum implementation → refactor only after green**; no drive-by changes; design gaps route back to `/tech-spec` or `/prd`.
- **Process expanded from 5 → 8 steps** to make TDD ordering binding (it previously collapsed "Implement, run tests, run lint" into a single step that allowed implement-first-then-test). New ordering: re-read inputs → state plan → **add failing tests** → minimum implementation → refactor → lint/typecheck → snapshot update → PROMPT_HISTORY update.

### 6. Review / critic command — **not added**
There is no review or critic command in `.rulesync/commands/`. The existing critic role is a **subagent** at `.rulesync/subagents/critic.md`, which is unchanged. Adding a new review/critic command would have introduced a new agent-like surface against your explicit constraint ("Avoid adding more subagents", "Do not add new agents unless absolutely necessary"). The "Critical reviewer" persona was therefore not embedded into a command file in this session — flagging this so you can decide whether to promote the subagent to a first-class command in a follow-up.

## Scope confirmation

```
M  .rulesync/commands/discovery.md
M  .rulesync/commands/implement.md
M  .rulesync/commands/prd.md
M  .rulesync/commands/tech-spec.md
M  .rulesync/commands/test-plan.md
M  docs/PROMPT_HISTORY.md                   (S006 row appended — ai-history rule)
?? docs/ai/sessions/S006-rulesync-command-personas.md   (new — ai-history rule)
 M .specstory/statistics.json               (auto-managed by SpecStory; not my edit)
?? .specstory/history/...rulesync-command-file-updates.md   (auto-captured raw transcript)
```

- **No product docs changed.** `docs/PRD.md`, `docs/discovery/NOTES.md`, `docs/PRD_TEMPLATE.md`, and the brief PDF are all untouched.
- **No implementation code changed.** Nothing under `app/`, `lib/`, `components/`, tests, or config files (`package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `biome.json`, etc.) was edited.
- **No new agents/subagents added.** `.rulesync/subagents/critic.md` is untouched and no new file was created under `.rulesync/`.
- **No phase-gate / rules / skills changes.** `.rulesync/rules/`, `.rulesync/skills/`, `.cursor/rules/`, and `AGENTS.md` are all untouched.
- The two `docs/` writes are **ai-history scaffolding only** (session snapshot + PROMPT_HISTORY row), as required by the project's `ai-history` user rule. Flagging in case you want them excluded from "no product docs" — they are not product artefacts but they do live under `docs/`, so let me know if you'd prefer me to revert them.

## Recommended next command

Run the repo's Rulesync generator so the new persona blocks are propagated into the tool-specific Cursor / Claude Code surfaces:

```bash
npm run rulesync:generate
```

(That's the `"rulesync:generate": "rulesync generate"` script defined in `package.json`.) Generated outputs are gitignored per S002, so the regeneration affects local-only files unless you revisit `.gitignore`.
